title: alnus incana var. hypomalaca
intro: Alnus incana var. hypomalaca, also known as the gray alder, is a variety of the alder tree native to Europe and Asia. The bark of this plant contains medicinal properties, including salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects. The health benefits of Alnus incana var. hypomalaca include reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in the treatment of arthritis, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the gray alder can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health issues.
